zrbfc_wait_build_completion 100 "Mirror"  # ~8 minutes at 5s intervals (image copy + about steps)
}

######################################################################
# Graft (graft vessel → GAR)

rbfd_graft() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Graft a locally-built image into GAR"
  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # No-arg: list available vessels
  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_sigils
    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
    buc_step "Available vessels:"
    local z_sigil=""
    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
    done
    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
  fi

  # Load and validate vessel
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "graft" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a graft vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Tweak override: test infrastructure can inject graft image via ambient regime variable
  test "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}" != "threemodegraft" || RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE}"

  test -n "${RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE not set for graft vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}'"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (graft about+vouch steps use tool images)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  local -r z_local_image="${RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE}"

  # No dirty-tree guard — image already built; git state irrelevant to container

  # Verify local image exists
  buc_step "Verifying local image exists"
  docker image inspect "${z_local_image}" > /dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "Local image not found: ${z_local_image} — build the image before grafting"
  buc_info "Local image confirmed: ${z_local_image}"

  # Extract image creation timestamp for hallmark T1
  buc_step "Reading image creation timestamp"
  local -r z_created_file="${ZRBFD_GRAFT_PREFIX}created.txt"
  docker image inspect --format '{{.Created}}' "${z_local_image}" > "${z_created_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to inspect image creation timestamp"
  local z_created=""
  z_created=$(<"${z_created_file}")
  test -n "${z_created}" || buc_die "Empty creation timestamp from docker inspect"
  buc_info "Image created: ${z_created}"

  # Parse ISO 8601 timestamp to YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Input formats: 2024-01-15T10:30:45.123456789Z or 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
  local z_created_clean="${z_created%%.*}"  # Remove fractional seconds
  z_created_clean="${z_created_clean%%Z}"   # Remove trailing Z if no fractional part
  z_created_clean="${z_created_clean%Z}"    # Handle edge case
  local -r z_cdate="${z_created_clean%%T*}"
  local -r z_ctime="${z_created_clean##*T}"
  local -r z_graft_ts="g${z_cdate:2:2}${z_cdate:5:2}${z_cdate:8:2}${z_ctime:0:2}${z_ctime:3:2}${z_ctime:6:2}"

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # GAR coordinates
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_base="${z_gar_host}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Generate push timestamp (T2) for hallmark
  local -r z_push_ts_file="${ZRBFD_GRAFT_PREFIX}push_ts.txt"
  date -u +'%y%m%d%H%M%S' > "${z_push_ts_file}" || buc_die "Failed to generate push timestamp"
  local z_push_ts
  z_push_ts="r$(<"${z_push_ts_file}")"
  test -n "${z_push_ts}" || buc_die "Empty push timestamp from ${z_push_ts_file}"
  local -r z_hallmark="${z_graft_ts}-${z_push_ts}"
  local -r z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"

  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"

  # Tag and push
  buc_step "Logging into GAR"
  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_gar_host}" \
    || buc_die "GAR authentication failed"

90	// ── Yoke fan-out — inscribe-reliquary yoking all ordain-side vessels ─────────
91	//
92	// Yoke applies to conjure-mode vessels only — they are the ones whose
93	// Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images at Cloud Build time, so
94	// they need the stamp written into rbrv.env. Bind-mode and graft-mode
95	// vessels skip Cloud Build (bind pins an upstream digest; graft pushes a
96	// pre-built image), so the reliquary toolchain is not in their build path.
97	// `rbfl_yoke` enforces this by refusing non-conjure vessels.
98	//
99	// ccyolo has RBRV_RELIQUARY= in its rbrv.env (present, empty) — it is a
100	// conjure-mode vessel whose Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images,
101	// so it needs the stamp at ordain time. Included.
102	//
103	// rbev-bottle-plantuml has RBRV_RELIQUARY= as schema parity but is
104	// bind-mode. Excluded from yoke fan-out — bind mode does not use the
105	// reliquary toolchain.
106	//
107	// rbev-graft-demo also has RBRV_RELIQUARY= but graft-mode vessels do not use
108	// the reliquary toolchain (they push a pre-built image; no Dockerfile build).
109	// Excluded from yoke fan-out despite the presence of the field.
110	
111	const RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS: &[&str] = &[
112	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_SENTRY_TETHER,
113	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE,
114	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE,
115	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_JUPYTER,
116	    "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo",
117	    // rbev-bottle-plantuml excluded: bind mode does not use reliquary tool images
118	    // rbev-graft-demo excluded: graft mode does not use reliquary tool images
119	];
120	
121	// ── Hallmark-base locator construction ───────────────────────
122	//
123	// Module-local literals matching rbgc_Constants.sh values. Used to compose the
124	// airgap-bottle's RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR after the forge hallmark is captured.
125	// Orchestration writes a hallmark-namespace locator directly into the consumer
126	// vessel's rbrv.env; conjure resolves the locator at airgap-bottle build time.
127	// The forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's
128	// success — no separate enshrine validation step on the consumer.
129	
130	const RBTDRO_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS: &str = "rbi_hm";
131	const RBTDRO_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE: &str = "image";
132	
133	/// Slot 1 of the airgap-bottle vessel — the only base-image slot the airgap
134	/// supply chain populates from a hallmark.
135	const RBTDRO_AIRGAP_BASE_ANCHOR_VAR: &str = "RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR";
136	
137	// ── Reliquary stamp witness ──────────────────────────────────
138	
139	/// Filename of a vessel's rbrv.env (relative to the vessel directory).
140	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_RBRV_FILE: &str = "rbrv.env";
141	
142	/// Field name yoked by case 1 in each vessel rbrv.env. Presence (non-empty)
143	/// is the cross-case witness that case 1 ran.
144	const RBTDRO_FIELD_RBRV_RELIQUARY: &str = "RBRV_RELIQUARY";
145	
146	/// Stable vessel chosen for the case-1 witness probe: sentry-tether is the
147	/// first entry in `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` and is yoked on every conjure
148	/// path, so its rbrv.env always carries the stamp after case 1.
149	const RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR: &str = RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_SENTRY_TETHER;

input.old_string:
buc_doc_brief "Yoke a reliquary stamp into a conjure vessel's rbrv.env — validate both, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil — folio (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether)"
  buc_doc_param "stamp"  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260327172456)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel sigil required (folio)"
  test -n "${z_stamp}"  || buc_die "Reliquary stamp required (arg 1)"

  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_vessel}"
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"

  case "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" in
    conjure) ;;
    bind)    buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=bind; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Bind vessels pin an upstream digest (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
    graft)   buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=graft; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Graft vessels push a locally-built image (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
    *)       buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has unrecognized RBRV_VESSEL_MODE='${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}'" ;;
  esac
  buc_info "Vessel valid — mode=${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}, egress=${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}"

lines: // ── Yoke fan-out — inscribe-reliquary yoking all ordain-side vessels ─────────
-// Yoke applies to conjure-mode vessels only — they are the ones whose
-// Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images at Cloud Build time, so
-// they need the stamp written into rbrv.env. Bind-mode and graft-mode
-// vessels skip Cloud Build (bind pins an upstream digest; graft pushes a
-// pre-built image), so the reliquary toolchain is not in their build path.
-// `rbfl_yoke` enforces this by refusing non-conjure vessels.
-// ccyolo has RBRV_RELIQUARY= in its rbrv.env (present, empty) — it is a
-// conjure-mode vessel whose Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images,
-// so it needs the stamp at ordain time. Included.
-// rbev-bottle-plantuml has RBRV_RELIQUARY= as schema parity but is
-// bind-mode. Excluded from yoke fan-out — bind mode does not use the
-// rbev-graft-demo also has RBRV_RELIQUARY= but graft-mode vessels do not use
-// the reliquary toolchain (they push a pre-built image; no Dockerfile build).
-// Excluded from yoke fan-out despite the presence of the field.

+// The reliquary tool images feed the about/vouch pipeline in every mode
+// (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft); bind also uses skopeo for image-copy. Yoke
+// fan-out covers every vessel that ordain will visit.

content.subject: Phase 1a: Remove empty-RBRV_RELIQUARY tolerance in zrbfd_preflight_reliquary
content.description: Strike the `test -n "${z_reliquary}" || return 0` early-return on line 116 of rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh. Empty RBRV_RELIQUARY is now a hard fail at preflight time (kindle schema enforces min=1 anyway, so empty-tolerance was dead code that contradicted the policy).

content.subject: Phase 1b: Wire zrbfd_registry_preflight into rbfd_graft
content.description: rbfd_graft (line 1631) currently does not call zrbfd_registry_preflight before submitting. Add the call after authentication, mirroring rbfd_mirror (line 1424) and rbfd_build (line 1156).

content.subject: Phase 1c: Open yoke mode-gate to bind and graft
content.description: rbfl_yoke (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh line 195) currently `buc_die`s on bind and graft modes with the message "Yoke only applies to conjure vessels." Replace the case statement so all three ordain modes accept yoke; only unrecognized modes die.

content.subject: Phase 1d: Fan out RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS to include plantuml and graft-demo
content.description: rbtdro_onboarding.rs declares `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` listing which vessel dirs the canonical-onboarding-sequence yokes after inscribe. Currently scoped to conjure vessels; extend to include the bind vessel (plantuml) and graft vessel (graft-demo).

content.subject: Phase 1: Build + fast-suite verify
content.description: After phase-1 code edits, run `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`. Watch-for: regime-validation failure on RBRV_RELIQUARY min=1 against post-rbw-MZ vessels (paddock flags this as adjacent-fix candidate).

**Code surface edits landed:**

| Edit | Location | Change |
|------|----------|--------|
| 1a | `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` `zrbfd_preflight_reliquary` (~L116) | Empty-tolerance return → `buc_die` with yoke remediation |
| 1b | `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` `rbfd_graft` (~L1704) | Added `zrbfd_registry_preflight` call after auth |
| 1c | `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` `rbfl_yoke` (L195) | Mode case now accepts conjure\|bind\|graft; doc_brief broadened |
| 1c+ | `rbz_zipper.sh` (L74) | RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY description → "ordain-path vessel" |
| 1d | `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` | Includes plantuml + graft-demo (uses existing constants) |
| 1d+ | `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` ordain-bind/ordain-graft probe | Now probes `reliquary stamp captured` (matches conjure pattern) |
| 1d+ | `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` ordain-bind docstring | Removed "no reliquary, no Cloud Build" stale claim |

**Verification:**
- bash -n: green on all 3 edited shell files
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`: green
- Fast suite: same 1 failure as BBAAs baseline (`rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` — pre-existing, paddock-flagged)

**Decisions notable for review:**
- I did the edits directly rather than delegate — each was a 1-3 line precise change; subagent overhead would dwarf the actual cost.
- Probe alignment for bind/graft (governor → reliquary) wasn't in the literal surface list but is load-bearing: bind/graft now require reliquary, and the conjure cases already use the reliquary probe transitively for governor presence. Consistency over keeping a stale narrower probe.
- I left `rbtdro_probe_governor_rbra` in place — still used by inscribe-reliquary case where it's the correct precondition.

Phase 2 (spec sweep) is the natural next step — that's RBSDY direct edit + RBS0 grep-driven discovery + RBSAC/RBSAB/RBSAG procedural specs. Want me to proceed?

params.intent: BBAA4 reliquary-universal-required phase 1 — code surfaces. zrbfd_preflight_reliquary's empty-RBRV_RELIQUARY tolerance (test -n || return 0) replaced with buc_die naming the yoke remediation; the early-return contradicted the kindle-schema min=1 and let bind/graft slip through to Cloud Build with no reliquary. rbfd_graft now calls zrbfd_registry_preflight after auth, mirroring rbfd_mirror and rbfd_build — graft's about/vouch pipeline consumes reliquary tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) just like the other modes. rbfl_yoke mode-gate accepts all three ordain modes (conjure|bind|graft); the per-mode buc_die messages claiming bind/graft don't use reliquary were lies and are removed. rbfl_yoke doc_brief broadened from 'conjure vessel' to 'vessel'. rbz_zipper's RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY description broadened identically — flows through tt context regeneration. rbtdro_onboarding.rs RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS now includes RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML and RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT (using existing constants); the comment block above replaced with the universal-reliquary explanation. ordain-bind and ordain-graft case probes switched from probe_governor_rbra to probe_reliquary_stamp, matching the conjure cases (case 1's success establishes governor transitively, and reliquary-yoked is now load-bearing for all ordain modes). ordain-bind docstring stripped 'no reliquary, no Cloud Build' stale claim — bind does use both via skopeo+about. cross-case probe-stamp docstring 'every conjure-path vessel' broadened to 'every ordain-path vessel'. probe_governor_rbra retained — still the correct precondition for inscribe-reliquary (case 1). Verification: bash -n green on all 3 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh enrollment-validation 47/47 + regime-validation 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAAs baseline — exactly the marshal-zero validator surfacing that paddock watch-for anticipates). Spec/handbook sweep deferred to phase 2 of this pace.

795	This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
796	All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
797	The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
798	Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
799	+
800	In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
801	+
802	Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
803	The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
804	+
805	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
806	The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).
807	
808	[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
809	//axl_voices axk_premise
810	{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
811	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
812	The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
813	Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
814	Batched invest is not supported — by design.

file.content:
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.

1730	An ark reaches the repository through one of three arrival paths:
1731	+
1732	* *Conjure*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job builds the image (context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}) and embeds about steps (via stitch), producing both the `image` and `about` packages. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verifies SLSA provenance via DSSE envelope signature verification in a separate Cloud Build job.
1733	* *Bind*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job copies the pinned upstream image and runs about steps, producing both the `image` and `about` packages. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} records a digest-pin verdict in a separate Cloud Build job.
1734	* *Graft*: Local docker/podman push places the `image` package. Then a single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job runs about steps followed by vouch steps, producing both `about` and `vouch` packages in one submission.
1735	+
1736	Conjure and bind each use two Cloud Build round-trips (primary job + vouch job). Graft uses one (combined about+vouch job), since the image arrives via local push rather than a Cloud Build step.
1737	Vouch is mandatory: an ark without a vouch artifact is incomplete.
1738	Arks are abjured atomically by
1739	{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.

file.content:
An ark reaches the repository through one of three arrival paths:
+
* *Conjure*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job builds the image (context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}) and embeds about steps (via stitch), producing both the `image` and `about` packages. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verifies SLSA provenance via DSSE envelope signature verification in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Bind*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job copies the pinned upstream image and runs about steps, producing both the `image` and `about` packages. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} records a digest-pin verdict in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Graft*: Local docker/podman push places the `image` package. Then a single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job runs about steps followed by vouch steps, producing both `about` and `vouch` packages in one submission.
+
Conjure and bind each use two Cloud Build round-trips (primary job + vouch job). Graft uses one (combined about+vouch job), since the image arrives via local push rather than a Cloud Build step.
Vouch is mandatory: an ark without a vouch artifact is incomplete.
Arks are abjured atomically by
{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.

[[rbtga_hallmark]]
//axl_voices axtg_timestamp
{rbtga_hallmark}::
The identifier marking when a vessel was hallmarked into an

lines: The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
-In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.

content.subject: Phase 2a: RBSDY — strike Gate-on-Vessel-Mode step, broaden NOTE
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc currently has a "Gate on Vessel Mode" step that fails on bind/graft and a NOTE saying yoke applies only to conjure. Strike the gating step entirely, broaden the NOTE to declare yoke applies to all ordain-path modes (conjure, bind, graft) since the about/vouch pipeline consumes reliquary tool images uniformly.

content.subject: Phase 2b: RBS0 — discovery-driven sweep of conjure-only reliquary claims
content.description: Run `grep -ni "conjure" Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -iE "reliquary|build step|tool"` per docket discovery recipe. Expect at least: (a) gcloud-tool NOTE claiming reliquary tools exist for conjure shell convenience (wrong — about+vouch use them in every mode), (b) GCB-step-contexts enumeration `(conjure, bind, enshrine)` missing graft. Broaden each site to reflect mode-uniform reliquary consumption.

content.subject: Phase 2c: RBSAC/RBSAB/RBSAG — declare universal reliquary precondition
content.description: Procedural specs RBSAC-ark_conjure, RBSAB-ark_about, RBSAG-ark_graft each need a universal reliquary precondition statement: every ordain-path vessel must have RBRV_RELIQUARY non-empty before the procedure can run; about/vouch pipeline depends on it across all modes. Match phrasing across the three so they tell one story.

content.text: Let me broaden the sweep — look for any remaining mode-specific reliquary claims beyond the docket recipe.

content.content:
1	//axhrb_regime
2	{rbrv_regime}
3	
4	This regime specifies variables that define container vessel configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrv_regime}
7	instance represents a vessel image source: bound from an external registry, conjured from a Dockerfile, or grafted from a local build.
8	RBRV assignment files are found in `rbev-vessels/*/rbrv.env` and configure image sourcing for bottle deployments.
9	
10	//axhrv_variable
11	{rbrv_sigil}
12	
13	Unique identifier for the vessel.
14	Must be 1-64 characters forming a valid cross-context identifier.
15	Must match the vessel directory name.
16	
17	//axhrv_variable
18	{rbrv_description}
19	
20	Human-readable description of the vessel purpose.
21	Optional; 0-512 characters.
22	
23	//axhrv_variable
24	{rbrv_user}
25	
26	Runtime user for the vessel container.
27	Optional; 0-64 characters.
28	When set, declares the non-root user that downstream orchestration (compose user directive, SSH authentication, exec sessions) should target.
29	When unset, the container runs as its image default.
30	Applies across all vessel modes: conjure vessels create the user in their Dockerfile; bind and graft vessels declare a user that already exists in the image.
31	
32	//axhrv_variable
33	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
34	
35	Selects vessel provisioning strategy.
36	Must be
37	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind},
38	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure},
39	or
40	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}.
41	
42	//axhrgv_variable
43	{rbrv_egress_mode}
44	
45	Selects which worker pool the primary build operation uses.
46	Must be `tether` (public egress) or `airgap` (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
47	Inscribe and enshrine always use tether regardless of this setting.
48	About and vouch always use airgap regardless of this setting.
49	Conjure and bind use the pool selected by this variable.
50	
51	//axhrgb_group
52	{rbrv_group_binding}
53	
54	//axhrgc_gate
55	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
56	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
57	
58	Variables in this group apply only when
59	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
60	is
61	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}.
62	Binding retrieves a pre-built image from an external registry without local build steps.
63	
64	//axhrgv_variable
65	{rbrv_bind_image}
66	
67	Fully-qualified image reference to copy from an external registry.
68	Must be a valid FQIN; 1-512 characters.
69	
70	//axhrgv_variable
71	{rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile}
72	
73	Optional path to reference Dockerfile for the upstream image.
74	When configured, the Dockerfile content is passed to
75	{rbtgo_ark_about}
76	and embedded in the about artifact as `recipe.txt`.
77	Optional; 0-512 characters.
78	
79	//axhrgb_group
80	{rbrv_group_conjuring}
81	
82	//axhrgc_gate
83	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
84	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
85	
86	Variables in this group apply only when
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	is
89	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
90	Conjuring builds a vessel image locally from a Dockerfile and build context in the repository.
91	
92	//axhrgv_variable
93	{rbrv_conjure_dockerfile}
94	
95	Path to Dockerfile relative to repository root.
96	Must be 1-512 characters.
97	
98	//axhrgv_variable
99	{rbrv_conjure_bldcontext}
100	
101	Build context path relative to repository root.
102	Must be 1-512 characters.
103	
104	//axhrgv_variable
105	{rbrv_conjure_platforms}
106	
107	Space-separated list of target platforms for multi-platform builds.
108	Must be 1-512 characters.
109	Examples: `linux/amd64`, `linux/amd64 linux/arm64`.
110	
111	//axhrgb_group
112	{rbrv_group_image}
113	
114	//axhrgc_gate
115	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
116	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
117	
118	Variables in this group declare base image dependencies for multi-stage Dockerfiles.
119	Up to 3 indexed slots (n = 1, 2, 3).
120	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
121	resolves each
122	{rbrv_image_origin}
123	to a GAR-mirrored
124	{rbrv_image_anchor}.
125	
126	//axhrgv_variable
127	{rbrv_image_origin}
128	
129	Upstream base image tag declaration (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`).
130	Vessel author declares intent; the {at_foundry} resolves to a concrete reference.
131	Optional; 0-512 characters per slot.
132	
133	//axhrgv_variable
134	{rbrv_image_anchor}
135	
136	GAR-mirrored, content-addressed anchor for the base image.
137	Written by
138	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
139	Format: `{sanitized-origin}-{10-char-sha256}`.
140	When present, conjure substitutes the full GAR reference.
141	When absent, conjure passes
142	{rbrv_image_origin}
143	through as the upstream tag.
144	Optional; 0-512 characters per slot.
145	
146	//axhrgb_group
147	{rbrv_group_grafting}
148	
149	//axhrgc_gate
150	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
151	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}
152	
153	Variables in this group apply only when
154	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
155	is
156	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}.
157	Grafting accepts a locally-built image pushed to GAR via native docker/podman push.
158	No Cloud Build involvement for the image itself — about and vouch run in Cloud Build after push.
159	
160	//axhrgv_variable
161	{rbrv_graft_image}
162	
163	Local image reference for graft operations.
164	Must be 1-512 characters.
165	Provides the image argument to
166	{rbtgo_ark_graft}.
167	
168	//axhrgv_variable
169	{rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile}
170	
171	Optional path to Dockerfile used for the local build.
172	When configured, the Dockerfile content is passed to
173	{rbtgo_ark_about}
174	and embedded in the about artifact as `recipe.txt`.
175	Optional; 0-512 characters.
176	
177	//axhro_kindle
178	{rbkro_kindle}
179	
180	Sets defaults for all RBRV_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
181	Detects unexpected RBRV_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
182	Builds ZRBRV_ROLLUP serialization string.
183	
184	//axhro_validate
185	{rbkro_validate}
186	
187	Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
188	Checks mode-appropriate presence invariant: RBRV_BIND_IMAGE for bind, RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE for conjure, optional RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE for graft.
189	Validates optional Dockerfile paths when configured: RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE (bind), RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE (graft).
190	
191	//axhro_render
192	{rbkro_render}
193	
194	Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
195	Sections: Core Vessel Identity, Egress Mode, Binding Configuration, Conjuring Configuration, Grafting Configuration.
196	Displays unexpected variables in red.
197	Validates after full render completes.
198	
199	//axhro_list
200	{rbkro_list}
201	
202	Enumerates available vessel instances by scanning subdirectories of RBRR_VESSEL_DIR for rbrv.env files.
203	Returns sigil identifiers suitable for instance selection.
204

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation conjures an
5	{rbtga_ark_image}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by building a container image from a vessel specification. The
9	{rbtr_director}
10	performs the build via
11	{gcb_service}
12	using direct `builds.create` API submission.
13	
14	NOTE: Conjure produces both the image and about artifacts in a single Cloud Build job submitted via `builds.create`.
15	The about steps (`rbgja01`-`rbgja04`) are embedded via stitch alongside the build steps.
16	The vouch artifact is created by the subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline stage (always a separate Cloud Build job).
17	
18	NOTE: The
19	{rbtga_hallmark}
20	is minted locally by the
21	{rbtr_director}
22	before submission.
23	The Director knows the complete artifact tag set before the build begins -- naming authority and verification authority are the same entity.
24	
25	NOTE: The conjure build job runs on the worker pool selected by the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`: tether pool for vessels needing public internet (e.g., `apt-get` in Dockerfile), airgap pool for vessels with all dependencies enshrined in GAR.
26	The subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} always runs on the airgap pool regardless of vessel egress mode.
27	
28	The conjure operation:
29	
30	//axhopt_typed_parameter
31	{rbtga_ark}
32	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
33	.. {rbbc_require}
34	vessel provided as first argument
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	vessel contains conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Registry Preflight*:
40	.. *Reliquary check*: HEAD request to `«RELIQUARY»/docker:latest` manifest in
41	{gar_service}.
42	{rbbc_require}
43	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run inscribe first.
44	The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images inscribed from upstream into private GAR. Each vessel declares its reliquary; multiple datestamped reliquaries can coexist.
45	.. *Enshrine check*: For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where both
46	{rbrv_image_origin}
47	and
48	{rbrv_image_anchor}
49	are set:
50	... Parse
51	{rbrv_image_anchor}
52	as a
53	{rbst_locator}
54	into package-path and tag
55	... HEAD request to `«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»` manifest in
56	{gar_service}
57	... {rbbc_require}
58	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} first.
59	Multiple vessels sharing the same anchor need only one enshrine.
60	.. Preflight prevents wasted
61	{gcb_service}
62	submission time on missing images, with copy-paste remediation commands
63	
64	//axhos_step
65	. *Resolve Base Images*:
66	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
67	{rbrv_image_origin}
68	is set:
69	... If
70	{rbrv_image_anchor}
71	is present: parse the
72	{rbst_locator}
73	and resolve to full GAR image reference (`«GAR-REPO-BASE»/«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»`)
74	... If
75	{rbrv_image_anchor}
76	is absent: pass
77	{rbrv_image_origin}
78	through as the upstream tag (development path)
79	.. Resolved references become `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args for the Dockerfile `ARG`/`FROM` pattern
80	
81	//axhos_step
82	. *Assign Hallmark*:
83	.. Mint
84	{rbtga_hallmark}
85	locally on the Director's workstation
86	.. {rbbc_store}
87	`«HALLMARK»` -- complete artifact tag set is now known before build submission
88	
89	//axhos_step
90	. *Push Pouch*:
91	.. Package build context (Dockerfile and context files) as a FROM SCRATCH OCI image
92	.. Push to GAR tagged `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/pouch:«HALLMARK»`
93	.. {rbbc_require}
94	push succeeds -- the pouch delivers build context to
95	{gcb_service}
96	
97	//axhos_step
98	. *Execute Build*:
99	.. Stitch
100	{rbtgr_build_json}
101	with all step image references and substitution variables
102	.. {rbbc_submit}
103	`POST «GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL»` with Build resource JSON via `builds.create`
104	.. All build parameters in stitched
105	{rbtgr_build_json}
106	-- zero custom substitution overrides
107	.. Waits for build completion
108	.. On success, the
109	{rbtga_ark_image}
110	artifact is created with the
111	{rbtga_hallmark}
112	.. {rbtgr_provenance}
113	is auto-generated by Google as a signed attestation on the built image
114	
115	//axhoot_typed_output
116	{rbtga_ark_image}
117	
118	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
119	{rbf_fact_hallmark}
120	The full
121	{rbtga_hallmark}
122	string.
123	
124	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
125	{rbf_fact_build_id}
126	The
127	{gcb_build}
128	job ID from the `builds.create` submission.
129	Used by
130	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
131	for
132	{rbtgr_provenance}
133	verification.
134	
135	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
136	{rbf_fact_gar_root}
137	{gar_service}
138	registry prefix.
139	Composable with
140	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
141	for full artifact references.
142	
143	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
144	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
145	Hallmark subtree prefix joining
146	{gar_service}
147	root with the hallmark path segment as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»`.
148	Composable with basename and `:«HALLMARK»` tag for full artifact references.
149	
150	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
151	{rbf_fact_ark_yield}
152	Per-platform
153	{rbtga_ark_image}
154	reference.
155	One fact file per platform discovered during the build.
156	
157	. *Display Results*:
158	.. {rbbc_show}
159	vessel image built with hallmark tag
160	
161	//axhoc_completion
162	Successful execution creates the
163	{rbtga_ark_image}
164	in
165	{rbtgi_repository}.
166	The {rbtr_director} retains `«BUILD_ID»` and `«HALLMARK»` from the conjure round-trip.
167	The `builds.create` job produces both the image and about artifacts under the hallmark subtree (about steps embedded via stitch).
168	{rbtgo_ark_ordain} chains only {rbtgo_ark_vouch} after the combined job completes, passing `«BUILD_ID»` for DSSE provenance verification.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_about}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation assembles the
5	{rbtga_ark_about}
6	metadata artifact for an existing
7	{rbtga_ark_image}
8	in
9	{rbtgi_repository}.
10	The about scripts (`rbgja01`–`rbgja04`) are uniform across all three vessel modes and always execute as Cloud Build steps.
11	This procedure describes those steps — not a standalone Cloud Build job.
12	Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft combines these steps with vouch steps in a single about+vouch Cloud Build job.
13	All jobs run as the
14	{rbtr_mason}
15	service account on the private pool (`RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`).
16	
17	NOTE: The about steps receive their context via substitution variables (`_RBGA_*`) and workspace state, not via function parameters.
18	For conjure, the hallmark is read from `/workspace` (computed by the preceding build steps); for bind and graft, it comes from the `_RBGA_HALLMARK` substitution variable.
19	See <<rbtgo_about_substitutions>> below for the full variable reference.
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Cloud Build Step 1: Discover Platforms* (Python 3):
23	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
24	.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
25	.. {rbbc_call}
26	Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` with OCI media type accept headers
27	.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
28	... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
29	OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
30	Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
31	They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
32	Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
33	+
34	See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
35	... {rbbc_store}
36	`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
37	.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
38	... {rbbc_call}
39	GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
40	... {rbbc_store}
41	`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
42	.. {rbbc_store}
43	platform count for subsequent step branching
44	.. After platform discovery, attempt diags artifact extraction (conjure-only):
45	... {rbbc_call}
46	Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
47	... If HTTP 200: fetch manifest, iterate layer blobs, extract diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`) to `/workspace`
48	... If HTTP 404 or error: skip gracefully (expected for bind/graft — no diags artifact exists)
49	.. NOTE: The diags artifact is pushed by conjure step 06 (`rbgjb06-push-diags.sh`) and contains build-time diagnostics that cannot be conveyed via Cloud Build substitution variables (Dockerfile content exceeding the ~4KB limit, BuildKit metadata, Docker daemon cache snapshots).
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Cloud Build Step 2: Syft SBOM Generation*:
53	.. Image: docker (from reliquary; runs syft via `docker run` with reliquary syft image)
54	.. Fetch OAuth token via metadata server (syft authenticates as {rbtr_mason})
55	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
56	... Conjure with per-platform tags (multi-platform): {rbbc_call}
57	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»-«PLATFORM_SUFFIX»`
58	... Conjure single-platform (single manifest, not an index): {rbbc_call}
59	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
60	... Bind/graft (always via per-platform digest): {rbbc_call}
61	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` using per-platform digest from step 1's manifest list
62	+
63	NOTE: Bind and graft images always use `@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` pinning, even for single-platform images.
64	Grafted images may be stored as OCI image indexes containing attestation manifests (see step 1 filtering); scanning the main tag would cause syft to auto-select the Cloud Build worker's native platform (amd64) from the index, failing when the image contains only a different architecture.
65	Digest pinning bypasses index platform resolution entirely.
66	... {rbbc_store}
67	per-platform SBOM to `/workspace/sbom-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `sbom-amd64.json`; LABEL = architecture + variant without separators)
68	.. NOTE: Syft's `registry:` transport reads directly from the OCI registry — no docker CLI or local pull required.
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Cloud Build Step 3: Assemble build_info.json* (Python 3):
72	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
73	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
74	... Construct `build_info.json` with mode-aware fields organized into nested JSON objects:
75	.... Root: `hallmark`, `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`
76	.... `git` object: `repo`, `branch`, `commit`
77	.... Conjure `build` object: `build_id` (from `_RBGA_BUILD_ID`), `inscribe_timestamp`, `qemu_used`
78	.... Conjure `slsa` object: `build_level`, `build_invocation_id`, `provenance_predicate_types`, `provenance_builder_id`
79	.... Bind `bind` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE`)
80	.... Graft `graft` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE`)
81	... {rbbc_store}
82	per-platform `build_info.json` to `/workspace/build_info-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `build_info-amd64.json`)
83	.. Write `recipe.txt` using dual-source logic:
84	... If `recipe.txt` already exists in `/workspace` (written by diags extraction in step 1): skip substitution variable write, log that `recipe.txt` came from the diags artifact
85	... Else if `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is non-empty: {rbbc_store}
86	`recipe.txt` to `/workspace/recipe.txt` from substitution variable content
87	... Else: omit `recipe.txt`
88	.. NOTE: The {rbtr_director} reads the Dockerfile locally and passes its content as `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT`.
89	If the file exceeds the Cloud Build substitution variable size limit (~4KB), the director omits it with a warning.
90	The diags path (conjure only) bypasses this limit by conveying the full Dockerfile via a registry artifact.
91	`_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is retained for all modes as a fallback when diags extraction fails or is absent.
92	
93	//axhos_step
94	. *Cloud Build Step 4: Build and Push About Artifact*:
95	.. Image: docker (from reliquary)
96	.. Create buildx builder instance (inspect-or-create pattern)
97	.. Build multi-platform `FROM scratch` container with per-platform SBOM, `build_info.json`, `recipe.txt` (when present), and conjure enrichment files when forwarded from the diags artifact: `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json` (conditional COPY — included only if present in `/workspace`)
98	.. For single-platform images: build single-platform about container
99	.. For multi-platform images: build with `buildx --push` using `TARGETARCH`/`TARGETVARIANT` to select per-platform files
100	.. Push to registry tagged `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»` via `docker buildx build --push`
101	.. {rbbc_require}
102	push succeeds
103	
104	//axhoc_completion
105	Successful execution of these four Cloud Build steps produces a
106	{rbtga_ark_about}
107	artifact in the registry tagged as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»`.
108	The about artifact is a multi-platform container matching the image artifact's platform list.
109	+
110	*Re-about idempotency*: Re-running about re-executes Syft and rebuilds `build_info.json`, overwriting the previous about artifact.
111	SBOM content may vary if Syft discovers different packages (e.g., after a Syft version update).
112	+
113	*Implementation note*: About and vouch share common Build resource JSON construction patterns (worker pool, mason SA, registry coordinates).
114	The implementation should extract a shared helper for `builds.create` request assembly.
115	
116	//axhos_waymark
117	[[rbtgo_about_substitutions]]
118	==== About Substitution Variables
119	
120	All `_RBGA_*` substitution variables are passed in the `builds.create` request body.
121	
122	*Registry and identity*::
123	[cols="1,2"]
124	|===
125	| `_RBGA_GAR_HOST` | GAR Docker host. Source: regime constants.
126	| `_RBGA_GAR_PATH` | GAR repository path. Source: regime constants.
127	| `_RBGA_VESSEL` | Vessel sigil. Source: loaded vessel configuration.
128	| `_RBGA_HALLMARK` | Full hallmark string. Source: input parameter.
129	| `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` | Vessel mode (`conjure`, `bind`, or `graft`). Source: loaded vessel configuration.
130	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` | Ark basename for image artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` constant.
131	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` | Ark basename for about artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` constant.
132	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` | Ark basename for diags artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` constant.
133	|===
134	
135	*Git metadata*::
136	[cols="1,2"]
137	|===
138	| `_RBGA_GIT_COMMIT` | Main repo HEAD SHA at operation time.
139	| `_RBGA_GIT_BRANCH` | Main repo branch at operation time.
140	| `_RBGA_GIT_REPO` | Main repo identifier (owner/repo).
141	|===
142	
143	*Mode-specific*::
144	[cols="1,2"]
145	|===
146	| `_RBGA_BUILD_ID` | Conjure Cloud Build job ID (conjure only). In the combined conjure job, populated from the built-in `$BUILD_ID`. In manual recovery standalone about, passed by the director from a prior conjure run. Used by step 3 to populate `build_info.json`. Empty for bind/graft.
147	| `_RBGA_INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP` | Inscribe timestamp from hallmark (conjure only). Empty for bind/graft.
148	| `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE` | Pinned upstream image reference (bind only). Source: {rbrv_bind_image}. Empty for conjure/graft.
149	| `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE` | Local image reference at push time (graft only). Empty for conjure/bind.
150	| `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` | Dockerfile content read locally by {rbtr_director} (all modes, optional). Source: {rbrv_conjure_dockerfile} (conjure, always populated), {rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile} (graft, optional), {rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile} (bind, optional). Empty if file exceeds ~4KB substitution limit or path not configured. Written to `recipe.txt` in step 3.
151	|===
152	
153	NOTE: `«PLATFORMS»` is discovered in step 1 and passed to subsequent steps as a workspace variable (written to `/workspace/platforms.txt`), not as a substitution variable.
154

//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_ark_about}

NOTE: This operation assembles the
{rbtga_ark_about}
metadata artifact for an existing
{rbtga_ark_image}
in
{rbtgi_repository}.
The about scripts (`rbgja01`–`rbgja04`) are uniform across all three vessel modes and always execute as Cloud Build steps.
This procedure describes those steps — not a standalone Cloud Build job.
Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft combines these steps with vouch steps in a single about+vouch Cloud Build job.
All jobs run as the
{rbtr_mason}
service account on the private pool (`RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`).

NOTE: The about steps receive their context via substitution variables (`_RBGA_*`) and workspace state, not via function parameters.
For conjure, the hallmark is read from `/workspace` (computed by the preceding build steps); for bind and graft, it comes from the `_RBGA_HALLMARK` substitution variable.
See <<rbtgo_about_substitutions>> below for the full variable reference.

//axhos_step
. *Cloud Build Step 1: Discover Platforms* (Python 3):
.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
.. {rbbc_call}
Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` with OCI media type accept headers
.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
+
See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
... {rbbc_store}
`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
... {rbbc_call}
GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
... {rbbc_store}
`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
.. {rbbc_store}
platform count for subsequent step branching
.. After platform discovery, attempt diags artifact extraction (conjure-only):
... {rbbc_call}
Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
... If HTTP 200: fetch manifest, iterate layer blobs, extract diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`) to `/workspace`
... If HTTP 404 or error: skip gracefully (expected for bind/graft — no diags artifact exists)
.. NOTE: The diags artifact is pushed by conjure step 06 (`rbgjb06-push-diags.sh`) and contains build-time diagnostics that cannot be conveyed via Cloud Build substitution variables (Dockerfile content exceeding the ~4KB limit, BuildKit metadata, Docker daemon cache snapshots).

//axhos_step
. *Cloud Build Step 2: Syft SBOM Generation*:
.. Image: docker (from reliquary; runs syft via `docker run` with reliquary syft image)
.. Fetch OAuth token via metadata server (syft authenticates as {rbtr_mason})
.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
... Conjure with per-platform tags (multi-platform): {rbbc_call}
`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»-«PLATFORM_SUFFIX»`
... Conjure single-platform (single manifest, not an index): {rbbc_call}
`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
... Bind/graft (always via per-platform digest): {rbbc_call}
`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` using per-platform digest from step 1's manifest list
+
NOTE: Bind and graft images always use `@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` pinning, even for single-platform images.
Grafted images may be stored as OCI image indexes containing attestation manifests (see step 1 filtering); scanning the main tag would cause syft to auto-select the Cloud Build worker's native platform (amd64) from the index, failing when the image contains only a different architecture.
Digest pinning bypasses index platform resolution entirely.
... {rbbc_store}
per-platform SBOM to `/workspace/sbom-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `sbom-amd64.json`; LABEL = architecture + variant without separators)
.. NOTE: Syft's `registry:` transport reads directly from the OCI registry — no docker CLI or local pull required.

//axhos_step
. *Cloud Build Step 3: Assemble build_info.json* (Python 3):
.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
... Construct `build_info.json` with mode-aware fields organized into nested JSON objects:
.... Root: `hallmark`, `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`
.... `git` object: `repo`, `branch`, `commit`
.... Conjure `build` object: `build_id` (from `_RBGA_BUILD_ID`), `inscribe_timestamp`, `qemu_used`
.... Conjure `slsa` object: `build_level`, `build_invocation_id`, `provenance_predicate_types`, `provenance_builder_id`
.... Bind `bind` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE`)
.... Graft `graft` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE`)
... {rbbc_store}
per-platform `build_info.json` to `/workspace/build_info-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `build_info-amd64.json`)
.. Write `recipe.txt` using dual-source logic:
... If `recipe.txt` already exists in `/workspace` (written by diags extraction in step 1): skip substitution variable write, log that `recipe.txt` came from the diags artifact
... Else if `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is non-empty: {rbbc_store}
`recipe.txt` to `/workspace/recipe.txt` from substitution variable content
... Else: omit `recipe.txt`
.. NOTE: The {rbtr_director} reads the Dockerfile locally and passes its content as `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT`.
If the file exceeds the Cloud Build substitution variable size limit (~4KB), the director omits it with a warning.
The diags path (conjure only) bypasses this limit by conveying the full Dockerfile via a registry artifact.
`_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is retained for all modes as a fallback when diags extraction fails or is absent.

//axhos_step
. *Cloud Build Step 4: Build and Push About Artifact*:
.. Image: docker (from reliquary)
.. Create buildx builder instance (inspect-or-create pattern)
.. Build multi-platform `FROM scratch` container with per-platform SBOM, `build_info.json`, `recipe.txt` (when present), and conjure enrichment files when forwarded from the diags artifact: `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json` (conditional COPY — included only if present in `/workspace`)
.. For single-platform images: build single-platform about container
.. For multi-platform images: build with `buildx --push` using `TARGETARCH`/`TARGETVARIANT` to select per-platform files
.. Push to registry tagged `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»` via `docker buildx build --push`
.. {rbbc_require}
push succeeds

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution of these four Cloud Build steps produces a
{rbtga_ark_about}
artifact in the registry tagged as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»`.
The about artifact is a multi-platform container matching the image artifact's platform list.
+
*Re-about idempotency*: Re-running about re-executes Syft and rebuilds `build_info.json`, overwriting the previous about artifact.
SBOM content may vary if Syft discovers different packages (e.g., after a Syft version update).
+
*Implementation note*: About and vouch share common Build resource JSON construction patterns (worker pool, mason SA, registry coordinates).
The implementation should extract a shared helper for `builds.create` request assembly.

//axhos_waymark
[[rbtgo_about_substitutions]]
==== About Substitution Variables

All `_RBGA_*` substitution variables are passed in the `builds.create` request body.

*Registry and identity*::
[cols="1,2"]
|===
| `_RBGA_GAR_HOST` | GAR Docker host. Source: regime constants.
| `_RBGA_GAR_PATH` | GAR repository path. Source: regime constants.
| `_RBGA_VESSEL` | Vessel sigil. Source: loaded vessel configuration.
| `_RBGA_HALLMARK` | Full hallmark string. Source: input parameter.
| `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` | Vessel mode (`conjure`, `bind`, or `graft`). Source: loaded vessel configuration.
| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` | Ark basename for image artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` constant.
| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` | Ark basename for about artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` constant.
| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` | Ark basename for diags artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` constant.
|===

*Git metadata*::
[cols="1,2"]
|===
| `_RBGA_GIT_COMMIT` | Main repo HEAD SHA at operation time.
| `_RBGA_GIT_BRANCH` | Main repo branch at operation time.
| `_RBGA_GIT_REPO` | Main repo identifier (owner/repo).
|===

*Mode-specific*::
[cols="1,2"]
|===
| `_RBGA_BUILD_ID` | Conjure Cloud Build job ID (conjure only). In the combined conjure job, populated from the built-in `$BUILD_ID`. In manual recovery standalone about, passed by the director from a prior conjure run. Used by step 3 to populate `build_info.json`. Empty for bind/graft.
| `_RBGA_INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP` | Inscribe timestamp from hallmark (conjure only). Empty for bind/graft.
| `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE` | Pinned upstream image reference (bind only). Source: {rbrv_bind_image}. Empty for conjure/graft.
| `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE` | Local image reference at push time (graft only). Empty for conjure/bind.
| `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` | Dockerfile content read locally by {rbtr_director} (all modes, optional). Source: {rbrv_conjure_dockerfile} (conjure, always populated), {rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile} (graft, optional), {rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile} (bind, optional). Empty if file exceeds ~4KB substitution limit or path not configured. Written to `recipe.txt` in step 3.
|===

NOTE: `«PLATFORMS»` is discovered in step 1 and passed to subsequent steps as a workspace variable (written to `/workspace/platforms.txt`), not as a substitution variable.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_graft}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation grafts a locally-built container image into
5	{rbtgi_repository}
6	via native docker/podman push.
7	No Cloud Build involvement in the image push — the image is already built on the local workstation.
8	No crane dependency — docker tag + docker push is sufficient for locally-present images.
9	No dirty-tree guard — git state is irrelevant to an already-built container.
10	
11	NOTE: The name "graft" comes from botanical grafting — foreign material spliced onto rootstock.
12	The graft may take or may not.
13	The metaphor fits the trust posture: grafted images have no provenance chain and receive a GRAFTED verdict from
14	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}.
15	
16	NOTE: Graft uses whatever container runtime the `docker` command resolves to in PATH, matching other {rbtr_director}-side operations.
17	No separate regime variable governs runtime selection for graft.
18	
19	The
20	{rbtr_director}
21	grafts an image:
22	
23	//axhopt_typed_parameter
24	{rbtga_ark}
25	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
26	.. {rbbc_require}
27	vessel provided as first argument
28	.. {rbbc_require}
29	local image reference provided as second argument (or defaulted from {rbrv_graft_image})
30	.. {rbbc_require}
31	vessel mode is {rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Load Vessel Configuration*:
35	.. Source vessel configuration from `rbrv.env`
36	.. {rbbc_store}
37	`«VESSEL_SIGIL»` from {rbrv_sigil}
38	.. {rbbc_store}
39	`«VESSEL_MODE»` from {rbrv_vessel_mode}
40	.. {rbbc_require}
41	`«VESSEL_MODE»` is `graft`
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Verify Local Image Exists*:
45	.. {rbbc_call}
46	`docker image inspect «LOCAL_IMAGE_REF»` (or podman equivalent)
47	.. {rbbc_require}
48	image exists in local container runtime
49	.. {rbbc_store}
50	`«LOCAL_DIGEST»` from local image inspect
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«LOCAL_PLATFORMS»` from local image manifest (typically single-platform for local builds)
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Authenticate as
56	{rbtr_director}*:
57	.. Execute
58	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
59	.. {rbbc_store}
60	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Construct Hallmark*:
64	.. {rbbc_call}
65	query local container runtime for image creation timestamp via `docker image inspect` (the `Created` field — OCI Image Configuration spec, exposed at `.Created` by both docker and podman)
66	.. {rbbc_store}
67	`«IMAGE_CREATED»` as the image's creation timestamp, converted to `YYMMDDHHMMSS` format
68	.. {rbbc_store}
69	`«PUSH_TIMESTAMP»` as current UTC time (`YYMMDDHHMMSS`)
70	.. {rbbc_store}
71	`«HALLMARK»` as `g«IMAGE_CREATED»-r«PUSH_TIMESTAMP»`
72	.. T1 encodes when the image was created (a fact about the artifact); T2 encodes when it was pushed to GAR
73	.. NOTE: The `created` field may be `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` for reproducible builds. This produces `g700101000000-r...` — a true statement about the image, not an error.
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Tag and Push Image*:
77	.. {rbbc_call}
78	`docker login` to GAR registry with director credentials
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«REGISTRY_TAG»` as `«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
81	.. {rbbc_call}
82	`docker tag «LOCAL_IMAGE_REF» «REGISTRY_TAG»`
83	.. {rbbc_call}
84	`docker push «REGISTRY_TAG»`
85	.. {rbbc_require}
86	push succeeds
87	
88	//axhoot_typed_output
89	{rbtga_ark_image}
90	
91	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
92	{rbf_fact_hallmark}
93	The full
94	{rbtga_hallmark}
95	string.
96	
97	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
98	{rbf_fact_build_id}
99	Empty for graft — no
100	{gcb_build}
101	involvement in image push.
102	Written for uniform fact coverage.
103	
104	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
105	{rbf_fact_gar_root}
106	{gar_service}
107	registry prefix.
108	Composable with
109	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
110	for full artifact references.
111	
112	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
113	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
114	The hallmark subtree prefix `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»`.
115	Composable with basename (`image`, `about`, `vouch`, etc.) and the `:«HALLMARK»` tag for full artifact references.
116	
117	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
118	{rbf_fact_ark_yield}
119	Single-platform
120	{rbtga_ark_image}
121	reference.
122	One fact file (graft is single-platform only).
123	
124	. *Display Results*:
125	.. {rbbc_show}
126	graft complete with hallmark tag
127	.. {rbbc_show}
128	local image reference, registry tag, digest
129	
130	//axhoc_completion
131	Successful execution pushes the locally-built image to
132	{rbtgi_repository}
133	tagged as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` within the hallmark subtree.
134	The graft operation returns `«HALLMARK»` to {rbtgo_ark_ordain}, which chains the subsequent pipeline stages automatically:
135	{rbtgo_ark_about} and {rbtgo_ark_vouch} in a single combined `builds.create` job (about steps followed by vouch steps) to assemble metadata and record the GRAFTED verdict.
136	+
137	*No SLSA provenance*: Grafted images have no Cloud Build provenance.
138	The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline records a GRAFTED verdict, explicitly marking the absence of a provenance chain.
139	This is a deliberate trust boundary — grafted images are accepted into the system but carry less attestation than conjured images.
140	+
141	*Push atomicity*: `docker push` is atomic per manifest — a failed push (network timeout, authentication expiry) leaves no partial state in GAR.
142	The director can safely retry without cleanup.
143	+
144	*Single-platform only*: Graft is specified for single-platform images only.
145	`docker push` of a locally-present image pushes a single manifest.
146	The about pipeline's platform discovery step handles this correctly, producing a single-platform `about` basename under the hallmark subtree.
147	Multi-platform graft is not currently specified — `buildx --load` only loads one platform into the local daemon, so true multi-platform local images would require a different push mechanism.
148

content.subject: Phase 2a: RBSDY — strike Gate-on-Vessel-Mode step, broaden NOTE
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc currently has a "Gate on Vessel Mode" step that fails on bind/graft and a NOTE saying yoke applies only to conjure. Strike the gating step entirely, broaden the NOTE to declare yoke applies to all ordain-path modes (conjure, bind, graft) since the about/vouch pipeline consumes reliquary tool images uniformly.

content.subject: Phase 2b: RBS0 — discovery-driven sweep of conjure-only reliquary claims
content.description: Run `grep -ni "conjure" Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -iE "reliquary|build step|tool"` per docket discovery recipe. Expect at least: (a) gcloud-tool NOTE claiming reliquary tools exist for conjure shell convenience (wrong — about+vouch use them in every mode), (b) GCB-step-contexts enumeration `(conjure, bind, enshrine)` missing graft. Broaden each site to reflect mode-uniform reliquary consumption.

content.subject: Phase 2c: RBSAC/RBSAB/RBSAG — declare universal reliquary precondition
content.description: Procedural specs RBSAC-ark_conjure, RBSAB-ark_about, RBSAG-ark_graft each need a universal reliquary precondition statement: every ordain-path vessel must have RBRV_RELIQUARY non-empty before the procedure can run; about/vouch pipeline depends on it across all modes. Match phrasing across the three so they tell one story.

content.text:
Fast suite identical to phase 1 baseline. Phase 2 complete.

## Phase 2 summary

**Spec sweep edits:**

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` | Struck "Gate on Vessel Mode" step entirely; broadened NOTE to "ordain-path vessel" with mode-uniform reliquary explanation |
| `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` L800 | GCB step contexts `(conjure, bind, enshrine)` → `(conjure, bind, graft, enshrine)` |
| `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` L805-806 | gcloud reliquary NOTE: replaced "conjure build steps" framing with mode-uniform about/vouch consumption |
| `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` L1163 | Yoke spec entry: "conjure vessel" → "ordain-path vessel" |
| `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` L2824/L2854 | Historical RBRG-elimination comments: "at conjure time" → "at ordain time" |
| `RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc` | Added universal reliquary precondition NOTE |
| `RBSAB-ark_about.adoc` | Added universal reliquary precondition NOTE (consistent phrasing) |
| `RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc` | Added universal reliquary precondition NOTE (consistent phrasing) |
| `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` | Regenerated from zipper — yoke description picked up the broadened wording |

**Verification:**
- Surviving "conjure vessel" mentions audited: all are about genuinely conjure-specific behavior (enshrine, kludge, attest packages, diags artifacts) — out of scope.
- No surviving "no reliquary" or "Yoke only applies" claims anywhere in the tree.
- Fast suite: identical to phase 1 baseline (1 pre-existing fail, paddock-flagged).

**Adjacency surfaced (NOT fixed):**
- RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc does not document `RBRV_RELIQUARY` at all (kindle schema enrolls it under "Tool Image Reliquary" group; spec is silent). No `rbrv_reliquary` linked term exists in RBS0's mapping section. Genuine gap, but outside this pace's surface list. Worth a follow-up paddock note or its own pace.

Want to notch phase 2, then proceed to phase 3 (handbook tracks)?

params.intent: BBAA4 reliquary-universal-required phase 2 — spec sweep. RBSDY: struck 'Gate on Vessel Mode' step entirely (the bind/graft fatal-die that was layer-one of the contradiction); broadened head NOTE to declare yoke applies to ordain-path vessels (conjure, bind, graft) with mode-uniform reliquary consumption (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft uniformly; bind adds skopeo). RBS0: GCB-step-contexts enumeration corrected from '(conjure, bind, enshrine)' to '(conjure, bind, graft, enshrine)' — graft does have a Cloud Build step (combined about+vouch) so the no-gcloud-in-CB-steps premise applies to it too. gcloud reliquary tool NOTE rewritten from 'conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell' to mode-uniform about/vouch consumption with provenance-retrieval clause folded in; second NOTE line added stating bind additionally uses skopeo. Yoke spec-entry summary 'conjure vessel' → 'ordain-path vessel'. Two RBRG-elimination historical comments 'at conjure time' → 'at ordain time' for accuracy. RBSAC/RBSAB/RBSAG: added a uniform 'Universal reliquary precondition' NOTE block declaring RBRV_RELIQUARY non-empty as a precondition for every ordain-path vessel — conjure phrasing emphasizes registry preflight enforcement, about phrasing emphasizes the four steps execute on reliquary tool images regardless of mode, graft phrasing emphasizes that graft's about+vouch combined CB job consumes the same reliquary tools. Wording deliberately consistent across the three so they tell one story. rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — picks up phase-1's broadened RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY description. Audit: surviving 'conjure vessel' mentions in the tree all genuinely conjure-specific (enshrine restricted to vessels with base-image slots, kludge local Dockerfile build, attest per-platform packages, diags artifact extraction); no 'no reliquary' / 'Yoke only applies' claims remain. Adjacency surfaced not fixed: RBSRV doesn't enumerate RBRV_RELIQUARY at all and no rbrv_reliquary linked term exists in RBS0's mapping section — genuine doc gap, outside this pace's surface list. Verification: tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh identical to phase-1 baseline (47/47 enrollment + 25/26 regime, rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged); .adoc files don't go through theurge build. Spec, orchestration, implementation now tell one story; handbook tracks remain in phase 3.

The yoke tabtarget rbw-dY already exists — no new tabtarget needed. Operator should be guided to inscribe a reliquary, then yoke it into the vessel before ordain.
